reasons: Minsitthar directly prevents Arlott from freely using movement skills inside King's Calling.
This denies Arlott's core reset pattern with Vengeance.

proof.summary: Arlott wants to dash repeatedly onto marked targets, while Minsitthar's ultimate prevents enemies from using movement skills inside the field.
worksBestWhen: Minsitthar casts after Arlott commits into the team.
Allies focus Arlott while his dash options are restricted.
failureCases: Arlott waits outside the field and re-enters later.
Minsitthar misses Arlott or catches only low-value targets.

reasons: Khufra punishes Arlott when he tries to dash through the frontline.
Bouncing Ball can break Arlott's entry timing and protect squishy allies.

proof.summary: Arlott relies on Vengeance movement to reach marked targets, while Khufra can knock up enemies that attempt to blink across him.
worksBestWhen: Backline allies stand behind Khufra's anti-dash zone.
failureCases: Arlott marks and attacks from another angle.
Khufra uses Bouncing Ball before Arlott commits.

reasons: Diggie reduces the value of Arlott's crowd-control-based teamfight setup.
Time Journey can protect allies when Arlott tries to chain crowd control and dash resets.

proof.summary: Arlott wants to use crowd control and marks to create a reset chain, while Diggie can protect nearby allies with cleanse and control immunity during the engage.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Arlott's real commit.
Diggie stands close enough to the allies Arlott wants to collapse on.
failureCases: Arlott catches Diggie before Time Journey is used.

reasons: Valir can push Arlott away before he reaches the marked target.
Knockback and slows make it harder for Arlott to maintain dash-reset pressure.

proof.summary: Arlott wants to stick to enemies and refresh Vengeance, while Valir can knock him away and slow his approach.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Arlott's dash.
The team does not clump into Arlott's crowd-control angle.
failureCases: Valir wastes Searing Torrent before Arlott commits.

reasons: Kaja can suppress Arlott during his commit and pull him away from marked targets.
This prevents Arlott from continuing his reset chain.

proof.summary: Arlott wants to keep dashing through marked enemies, while Kaja can suppress and drag him out before the chain continues.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits until Arlott commits into the backline.
failureCases: Arlott baits Divine Judgment with a tanky entry.
Kaja is forced to use ultimate defensively on another threat.
